methods-and-responsibility.label: Methods and Responsibility
methods-and-responsibility.summary: Move behavior onto the object that owns the data, then let helper files format or coordinate results.

sketch2.title: Methods and Responsibility: what the grader checks

ci_open_task_list_practice.title: Filter a list of Task objects

try_methods_and_responsibility_sketch0.title: Try it yourself
try_methods_and_responsibility_sketch0.prompt: Complete `Task.summary()` so the object describes its own state.

ci-open-task-list-practice.title: Filter a list of Task objects
ci-open-task-list-practice.prompt: Complete `services/task_reports.py` so it builds lines for only open tasks.
ci-open-task-list-practice.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

ci-open-task-list-practice.starterCode:
from models.task import Task
from services.task_reports import open_task_lines

tasks = [Task("plan", False), Task("ship", True), Task("review", False)]
for line in open_task_lines(tasks):
    print(line)

sc-methods-and-responsibility-design.title: Check the design choice
sc-methods-and-responsibility-design.prompt: Which choice best matches the lesson goal for methods and responsibility?
sc-methods-and-responsibility-design.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Move behavior onto the object that owns the data, then let helper files format or coordinate results.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.a: Put every class in main.py so imports are never needed.
options.b: Give each class or service a focused responsibility and import it where needed.
options.c: Avoid methods so every object exposes all of its data directly.
options.d: Copy the same logic into each subclass to avoid shared code.

try-methods-and-responsibility-sketch0.title: Put summary behavior on the Task object
try-methods-and-responsibility-sketch0.prompt: Complete `Task.summary()` so the object describes its own state.
try-methods-and-responsibility-sketch0.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-methods-and-responsibility-sketch0.starterCode:
from models.task import Task

task = Task("write tests", True)
print(task.summary())

try-methods-and-responsibility-sketch1.title: Use a helper file for formatting
try-methods-and-responsibility-sketch1.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-methods-and-responsibility-sketch1.starterCode:
from models.product import Product

product = Product("pencil", 125)
print(product.label())

concept.title: Methods and Responsibility: the idea
concept.bodyMarkdown: **Methods and Responsibility** is not just syntax. In this course it is practiced inside small projects where one file owns one clear part of the program. Move behavior onto the object that owns the data, then let helper files format or coordinate results.

multifile-shape.title: Methods and Responsibility: the file shape
multifile-shape.bodyMarkdown: The file layout matters. Models hold object state and behavior, services coordinate groups of objects, tests verify behavior, and `main.py` stays thin enough to read.

graded-habit.title: Methods and Responsibility: what the grader checks
graded-habit.bodyMarkdown: The grader checks behavior from the running project. A strong answer keeps imports working, keeps all files present, and proves the object behavior through output or assertions.
